PR #3051 · orchestkit · 13 commits

When a green gate means nothing
the eval harness reported success for runs it never made

Every defect fixed here is the same shape: something reported success while measuring nothing. Exit 0 meant both "passed" and "never ran". A delta gate stayed green at 0%. An advisory check silently blocked a required one. Drive the controls below to see what the harness used to print versus what it prints now.

Verdict simulator

Pick a lane state. The left pane is the harness before this PR, the right is after. These are the real rules from run-skill-eval.sh and run-quality-eval.sh, not an illustration.

0%
0%

Before this PR

Trigger-
Quality-
Overall-
-

After this PR

Trigger-
Quality-
Overall-
-

What this PR changed

Counts are measured, not estimated.

4
verdict classes that reported green while measuring nothing
114
skills with deterministic checks, up from 31
37
non-latest model recommendations, previously invisible
5
false positives I generated and caught before shipping

The four lies

WhereReportedTruth
run-trigger-evalPassed: 1skipped, never ran
run-skill-eval:641Trigger: PASS ()empty score, exit 0 read as pass
run-quality-eval:1159Quality: PASS (0%)0% vs 0% is delta 0, so it passed
ci.yml summaryrequired check redan advisory job was in the blocking loop

Gates that did not exist yesterday

GateCatchesCost
test-skill-coveragedescription collisions, unreachable skills, trigger contradictions$0, 3s
test-model-recencya model that is valid but no longer latest for its channel$0, <10s
budget-governora run the weekly subscription cap cannot pay for$0
All three are ratchets with committed baselines. Counts may only go down, so pre-existing findings do not redden every PR on day one.
Generated for PR #3051. The simulator encodes the real branch logic: unmeasured lanes render N/A, a 0% vs 0% delta renders NO SIGNAL, and a died generation renders INCONCLUSIVE rather than inventing a verdict from an outage.
